title: Ensure that the --encryption-provider-config argument is set as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --encryption-provider-config argument is set as appropriate
input_row.rationale: etcd is a highly available key-value store used by Kubernetes deployments for persistent storage of all of its REST API objects. These objects are sensitive in nature and should be encrypted at rest to avoid any disclosures. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --encryption-provider-config argument is set to a EncryptionConfig file. Additionally, ensure that the EncryptionConfig file has all the desired resources covered especially any secrets.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and configure a EncryptionConfig file. Then, edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the master node and set the --encryption-provider-config parameter to the path of that file: --encryption-provider-config=</path/to/EncryptionConfig/File> Default Value: By default, --encryption-provider-config is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/encrypt-data/ 2. https://acotten.com/post/kube17-security 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kube- apiserver/ 4. https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/issues/92

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_encryption_provider_config_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate the kube-apiserver Pod specification, typically found in the static Pod manifest at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` (for clusters using static Pods), or via the Kubernetes API if the control plane is managed as a Pod.
- Use the Kubernetes API or `kubectl` to retrieve the kube-apiserver Pod spec:
  - Command: `kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o yaml`
  - Or, if not running as a Pod, programmatically access the static manifest file (requires host access, but in managed clusters, the Pod spec is available via the API).
- Inspect the `command` or `args` field for the kube-apiserver container to verify the presence of the `--encryption-provider-config` flag.
- Validate that the `--encryption-provider-config` flag is set and points to a valid file path (e.g., `/etc/kubernetes/encryption-config.yaml`).
- (Optional, advanced) If the encryption config file is mounted as a ConfigMap or Secret, enumerate the referenced resource to ensure it exists.
- Pass Criteria:
  - The kube-apiserver process (or Pod spec) includes the `--encryption-provider-config` argument with a non-empty value.
- Fail Criteria:
  - The `--encryption-provider-config` argument is missing or empty.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.11.1 PDF
